project_overview.project_name: Somalia Enhancing Public Resource Management Project (SERP)
project_overview.project_development_objective: To strengthen accountability, transparency and institutional capacity in public resource management, across participating entities
project_overview.total_financing: US$75 million (IDA Grant: US$60 million / SDR 46.8 million + Somalia Multi-Partner Fund 2: US$15 million)
project_overview.beneficiaries: Federal Government of Somalia (FGS), Federal Member States (Galmudug, Hirshabelle, Jubbaland, Puntland, Southwest), Government of Somaliland; primary beneficiaries include government institutions, civil servants, and system users in implementing MDAs; secondary beneficiaries include all Somali citizens

geographic_scope.fragile_or_conflict_affected_areas: yes - Somalia is classified as a fragile state; the entire country is affected by protracted conflict, fragility, and violence (FCV); south-central Somalia and Mogadishu face active Al-Shabaab insecurity; the project explicitly operates in a fragile, conflict-affected, and violent (FCV) environment

key_components.description: Strengthens budget preparation, execution, and oversight for FGS, FMSs, and SL. Includes: strengthening budget execution functions (legal/internal control frameworks, FMIS upgrades, procurement reforms, internal/external audit); PFM in health and education sectors; harmonization of PFM systems between FGS and FMSs; budget preparation and transparency; and climate-informed public investment management.
key_components.financing: US$22.5 million (FGS & FMSs: US$17.42 million; SL: US$5.08 million)

key_components.description: Supports DRM priorities of FGS, FMSs, and SL to strengthen inland revenue systems and capacity and harmonize tax administration. Includes: improving tax policy capacity; modernizing and automating inland revenue and customs administration (ITAS implementation, TIN harmonization, LTO reforms); capacity building and scholarships; and taxpayer education and facilitation.
key_components.financing: US$26 million (FGS & FMSs: US$19.5 million; SL: US$6.5 million)

key_components.description: Enhances systems, staff skills, and effectiveness of civil service management. Includes: strengthening capacity of central and line MDAs (strategic staffing, online administrative services pilot in SL, civil servant training, FGS-FMSs coordination); and implementing civil service management systems including HRMIS, HRM policies, pay and grading systems, and pension systems.
key_components.financing: US$14.5 million (FGS & FMSs: US$12.5 million; SL: US$2 million)

key_components.description: Supports interconnectivity and interoperability of PFM, DRM, and HR systems across FGS, FMSs, and SL; professionalization of PFM and DRM staff through functional reviews, competency frameworks, and skills gap analysis; and change management strategy to support DRM, PFM, and civil service reforms.
key_components.financing: US$4 million (FGS & FMSs: US$3.08 million; SL: US$0.92 million)

key_components.component_name: Component 5: Project Management, Coordination and Support in Delivery
key_components.description: Supports overall project management, coordination, and M&E. Includes establishment and operation of Project Coordination Unit (PCU) at FGS level and SL PCU; FMSs project managers; and results monitoring and impact evaluation including GEMS-based M&E system.
key_components.financing: US$8 million (FGS & FMSs: US$6 million; SL: US$2 million)

implementation_arrangements.implementing_agency: Federal Ministry of Finance, Somalia (lead); Ministry of Finance, Somaliland; FMSs Ministries of Finance and relevant MDAs including Civil Service Commissions, Ministries of Labour, Ministries of Planning, Offices of the Auditor General, Revenue Departments
implementation_arrangements.coordination_mechanisms: Project Steering Committee (PSC) at Director General level across FGS and FMSs; Project Coordination Unit (PCU) housed in FGS Ministry of Finance; separate SL Steering Committee (SSC) and SL PCU; FMSs project managers reporting to respective MoFs; coordination with Intergovernmental Fiscal Federalism Technical Meeting (IGFFTM); External Assistance Fiduciary Section (EAFS) units for FM; thematic technical working groups
implementation_arrangements.procurement_approach: World Bank Procurement Regulations for IPF Borrowers (July 2016, updated November 2020); all procurement subject to prior World Bank review regardless of value; STEP system for procurement tracking; procurement risk rated High (residual Substantial); dedicated Procurement Specialists in PCU, SL PCU, and Puntland MoF; joint ventures with local firms encouraged for international contracts
implementation_arrangements.monitoring_and_evaluation: GEMS (Geo-Enabling Initiative for Monitoring and Supervision) platform; PCU responsible for tracking results framework indicators; semi-annual implementation support missions; annual joint progress reviews; mid-term review; third-party monitoring for output verification; quarterly IFRs; independent impact evaluation under Component 5; gender-disaggregated reporting

identified_risks.risk_description: Political instability and contestation among key political and economic partners could slow or derail reforms. FGS-FMSs dialogue could be disrupted by FMSs election processes. New political settlement post-2022 presidential elections could weaken strategic support. Ongoing constitutional review process may cause resistance from groups seeking to maintain status quo.
identified_risks.mitigation_measures: Substantial consultation with multiple stakeholder categories beyond politically appointed leaders; leveraging preexisting dialogue platforms (IGFFTM, DRM forum); establishment of intergovernmental forum on civil service reform; change management activities throughout implementation; continued technical engagement even during politically sensitive periods

identified_risks.risk_description: Limited government capacity to respond to external shocks; climate shocks (drought, floods) lowering agricultural output and displacing people; COVID-19, protracted elections, severe drought, and rising global food/energy prices constraining growth; persistent cycle of shocks increasing vulnerability; economic activity exposed to insecurity risks in Mogadishu and south-central Somalia.
identified_risks.mitigation_measures: Project financed 100% by grants (no counterpart funding risk); fiscal affordability of reforms considered in planning; project supports DRM strengthening and medium-term fiscal planning to reduce macroeconomic risks

identified_risks.risk_description: Significant heterogeneity between strategies and policies across FGS and FMSs; fragmentation is a potential risk to harmonization agenda.
identified_risks.mitigation_measures: Highly consultative processes during implementation; strong dialogue with FGS, FMSs, and donors; regular technical thematic forums

identified_risks.risk_description: Broad geographic and technical scope creates risks related to dynamics between MDAs and between FGS, FMSs, and SL. Entities working together more closely in new ways creates friction and competition risks that could delay implementation.
identified_risks.mitigation_measures: PCU with support staff to lead coordination; FMSs empowered in coordination and decision-making; implementation aligned with existing institutional mandates; clarity on where FGS vs FMSs should lead implementation

identified_risks.risk_description: Limited institutional capacity of implementing agencies; public sector lacks skilled managerial and professional staff (missing middle); complex implementation and M&E arrangements across multiple jurisdictions.
identified_risks.mitigation_measures: Dedicated PSM component to enhance public sector systems and institutionalize capacity building; augmenting existing capacity with external support; structured approach to capacity building

identified_risks.risk_description: Weak control environment in FGS, FMSs, and SL; lack of key FM competencies; weak internal controls; reliance on consultants; lack of regulatory framework for key PFM aspects; limited banking sector isolated from global financial system; over-reliance on single correspondence bank; challenges in scholarship program fund verification; widespread fraud and corruption allegations; collusion of bidders; weak legal and financial institutions.
identified_risks.mitigation_measures: EAFS units charged with FM responsibility; integrated project plans and budgets; regular training of fiduciary staff; internal audit arrangements; detailed FM arrangements in POM for scholarship program; dedicated Procurement Specialists; prior World Bank review of all procurement; Probity Advisors for complex procurements; third-party monitoring; anti-corruption guidelines in all bidding documents

identified_risks.risk_description: Elite capture of project benefits; exclusion of vulnerable/marginalized groups from recruitment and training; labor risks including OHS, security risks, sexual harassment, and GBV in capacity building activities; contextual risks of operating in conflict zone; challenges for effective consultations, monitoring, and grievance redress; resistance from public officials wishing to maintain status quo of weaker systems.
identified_risks.mitigation_measures: ESMF, Labor Management Procedures, SEAH Prevention and Response Action Plan, Security Management Framework; environmental and social specialist at federal level; project GRM; conflict-sensitive implementation approach; monitoring of power dynamics and conflict drivers; GEMS for real-time E&S risk monitoring

identified_risks.risk_description: Complex coordination between national, sub-national, and MDA levels; win-lose situations where vested interests are incompatible; PFM and civil service salary reforms affect stakeholder interests; risk of delays or halting of activities.
identified_risks.mitigation_measures: Continuous engagement with all stakeholders and civil society; understanding relative power and influence of stakeholders; citizen feedback and redress mechanisms; change management strategy

identified_risks.risk_description: Prevailing security situation in Somalia poses risks to human security (World Bank and client), project implementation, and sustainability. Limitations on access to operating environment; additional project risks from insecurity; significant increase in project implementation costs due to security.
identified_risks.mitigation_measures: Close consultation with WBG Corporate Security and UN Department of Safety and Security; contracting with security firms in project-active areas; Security Management Framework; virtual supervision tools (GEMS, virtual meetings); third-party monitoring

safeguards_and_social.environmental_category: Substantial (Environmental and Social Risk Classification); Environmental risk: Moderate; Social risk: Substantial
triggered_safeguard_policies: ESS1: Assessment and Management of Environmental and Social Risks and Impacts
ESS3: Resource Efficiency and Pollution Prevention and Management
ESS6: Biodiversity Conservation and Sustainable Management of Living Natural Resources
ESS10: Stakeholder Engagement and Information Disclosure
safeguards_and_social.grievance_redress_mechanism: yes - Project-level GRM being designed for beneficiaries/affected communities (no cost, no discrimination); separate mechanism for project workers for labor-related complaints; both include appeals and escalation mechanisms; linked to GEMS for real-time monitoring; World Bank Grievance Redress Service (GRS) and Accountability Mechanism also available
safeguards_and_social.gender_considerations: Project is gender-tagged; promotes female representation in civil service through meritocratic recruitment; 50% of training seats reserved for women; gender-disaggregated results indicators including female staff at Director General/Director/Head of Section positions (target: 30% by end); monitoring of female participation in public hearings; tracking women accessing taxpayer information; citizen feedback mechanism with specific focus on female users in SL; GBV/SEAH Prevention and Response Action Plan required
vulnerable_groups: Women (underrepresented in formal labor force and civil service)
Persons with disabilities (universal access considerations for new buildings)
Persons living in poverty (69% of population below US$1.90/day PPP)

fcv_relevant_details.conflict_sensitivity: Project explicitly designed for FCV environment with conflict-sensitive approach; project team monitors potential impact of activities on power dynamics and conflict drivers; change management strategy addresses vested interests and resistance to reform; flexible project design enables rapid response to unforeseen needs; consultative and inclusive approach throughout preparation and implementation; project supports state legitimacy building and social contract renewal; integration of revenue, expenditure, and service delivery intended to reduce role of non-state actors; security management framework required; third-party monitoring used due to access constraints
fcv_relevant_details.fragility_context: Somalia classified as fragile state; over two decades of protracted conflict and state collapse; weak government capacity and low state legitimacy; asymmetric federal structures with nascent intergovernmental fiscal relations; informal taxation and fragmentation; clan-based political interference in civil service; Al-Shabaab active in south-central Somalia; multiple concurrent shocks (COVID-19, drought/famine, desert locusts, Ukraine war impacts); FGS revenue-to-GDP ratio only 3%; 69% population below poverty line; limited banking sector isolated from global financial system; HIPC interim period with completion point contingent on reforms; 7.1 million people food insecure; 1 million displaced by drought
fcv_relevant_details.security_considerations: High security risk rated for project; Al-Shabaab and COVID-19 create travel restrictions to project sites; World Bank operates with WBG Corporate Security and UN DOSS support; security firms contracted for day-to-day missions; Security Management Framework required as effectiveness condition; security costs significantly increase project implementation costs; limited interest from international contractors due to country security risk; virtual supervision tools (GEMS) used to compensate for access limitations; security risk analysis required before project effectiveness
fcv_relevant_details.displacement_or_refugees: yes - approximately 1 million people internally displaced due to severe drought conditions as rural dwellers seek food and basic services in urban areas; worsening drought since 2020 contributing to high food insecurity affecting 7.1 million people; Inter-Agency Standing Committee activated humanitarian system-wide Scale-Up protocols in August 2022 for drought/famine response; no specific refugee programming in this project but context of widespread internal displacement acknowledged
